title: “Mars Had Alien Life!” (Ft. Joe McMoneagle)
summary: Jesse Michels interviews Joseph McMoneagle, known as 'remote viewer number one' from the Stargate program, about his military service, near-death experiences in Vietnam, and his extensive work in remote viewing. The conversation covers the science behind psychic functioning, how remote viewing protocols work, factors that affect psychic abilities, and McMoneagle's claims about viewing structures on Mars.

acronyms.definition: A code used by the military to identify a specific job or role
Training that follows basic combat training to prepare soldiers for specific military jobs
Communist guerrilla force in South Vietnam during the Vietnam War
A retail store on a military installation

events.description: McMoneagle was blown out of a helicopter hit by an RPG in Vietnam, falling over 100 feet and surviving with compression fractures
Russell Targ and Hal Puthoff wrote an article for Nature magazine and later a book called Mind Reach about remote viewing

key_claims.claim: McMoneagle has approximately 60 photographs of things on Mars that are clearly alien
McMoneagle remote viewed a pyramid on Mars a million years ago
McMoneagle won the Legion of Merit for over 200 instances in which he added to Military Intelligence
Pat Price psychically described classified project names from files inside safes at a clandestine CIA site
When the sun is above the horizon and you're facing it, there is a fourfold reduction in psychic functioning
Intent can override all conditions that would normally block psychic functioning
Brain cells exist throughout the nervous system, not just in the skull
Russians trained psychics to target American Congressmen, Senators, cabinet members, and the president, but it did not work
The Amazing Randi's million dollar challenge was actually an investment that would pay out over 30 years, not a direct payment
